title: 〜くせに
description: 〜くせに means "even though," but unlike neutral concessives it always carries blame, criticism, or contempt toward the subject. Use it to point out that someone acts in a way that clashes with what they are or know.

content.text: 〜くせに links two clauses about the same person, where the second contradicts what you'd expect from the first — and you disapprove. It attaches like a な-adjective: verbs and い-adjectives in plain form, nouns with の, な-adjectives with な. 知（し）っているくせに = "even though (they) know."

content.japanese: Verb・い-adj （plain）／ Noun ＋ の ／ な-adj ＋ な　＋ くせに
知（し）っているくせに、教（おし）えてくれない。
content.english: Even though he knows, he won't tell me.

content.japanese: 子供（こども）のくせに、生意気（なまいき）だ。
content.english: He's cheeky for a little kid (even though he's just a kid).

content.japanese: お金（かね）がないくせに、高（たか）い物（もの）ばかり買（か）う。
content.english: Even though he has no money, he buys nothing but expensive things.

content.japanese: 下手（へた）なくせに、全然（ぜんぜん）練習（れんしゅう）しない。
content.english: Even though he's bad at it, he never practices.

content.text: 〜のに and 〜くせに both translate as "even though," but 〜のに is neutral surprise or disappointment, while 〜くせに adds a sneer. You generally cannot use 〜くせに about yourself, and both clauses must share the same subject — usually a person. Swap it in only when you mean to criticize.

content.japanese: 本当（ほんとう）は好（す）きなくせに、嫌（きら）いだと言（い）う。
content.english: Even though he actually likes it, he says he hates it.

content.text: Because 〜くせに is blunt and judgmental, avoid it in polite or formal situations — use 〜のに or 〜にもかかわらず instead. Also don't use it when the two clauses have different subjects; 〜のに handles those cases, but 〜くせに sounds wrong.

examples.japanese: Verb・い-adj （plain）／ Noun ＋ の ／ な-adj ＋ な　＋ くせに
知（し）っているくせに、教（おし）えてくれない。
examples.english: Even though he knows, he won't tell me.

examples.japanese: 子供（こども）のくせに、生意気（なまいき）だ。
examples.english: He's cheeky for a little kid (even though he's just a kid).

examples.japanese: お金（かね）がないくせに、高（たか）い物（もの）ばかり買（か）う。
examples.english: Even though he has no money, he buys nothing but expensive things.

examples.japanese: 下手（へた）なくせに、全然（ぜんぜん）練習（れんしゅう）しない。
examples.english: Even though he's bad at it, he never practices.

examples.english: Even though he actually likes it, he says he hates it.
